prevalence.source: NFHS-5 + national anemia control program context

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Low hemoglobin with microcytosis; ferritin/TSAT when available; clinical nutrition and infection assessment

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: programmatic screening under anemia control initiatives; varies by state
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; adherence and supply constraints affect outcomes

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Hemoglobin screening through public programs; iron studies limited
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Iron supplementation, deworming, nutrition support, and maternal anemia interventions
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High baseline prevalence, adherence, and supply-chain consistency
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Large-scale national anemia programs and low-cost supplementation
